request.prompt: a members table where each row has a remove button

children.text: Are you sure you want to remove Alice Smith? This will revoke their access to the workspace.
Are you sure you want to remove Bob Jones? This will revoke their access to the workspace.
Are you sure you want to remove Charlie Davis? This will revoke their access to the workspace.

findings.message: Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-cancel' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
findings.rationale: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.

findings.message: Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-title' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
findings.rationale: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.

findings.message: Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-title' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
findings.rationale: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.

findings.message: 'alert-dialog-trigger' contains no 'button' to carry the required content.
findings.rationale: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.

Your surface violates 9 governance rule finding(s) of the "shadcn/ui" design system:

Violation 1: [rule.alertdialog-requires-cancel / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[1].children[0].children[3].children[0] (component: alert-dialog)
  Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-cancel' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
  Why: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.
Violation 2: [rule.alertdialog-requires-cancel / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[1].children[0].children[3].children[0] (component: alert-dialog)
  Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-title' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
  Why: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.
Violation 3: [rule.alertdialog-requires-cancel / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[2].children[3].children[0] (component: alert-dialog)
  Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-cancel' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
  Why: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.
Violation 4: [rule.alertdialog-requires-cancel / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[2].children[3].children[0] (component: alert-dialog)
  Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-title' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
  Why: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.
Violation 5: [rule.alertdialog-requires-cancel / must]
  At: $.root.children[1].children[3].children[0] (component: alert-dialog)
  Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-cancel' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
  Why: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.
Violation 6: [rule.alertdialog-requires-cancel / must]
  At: $.root.children[1].children[3].children[0] (component: alert-dialog)
  Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-title' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
  Why: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.
Violation 7: [rule.trigger-carries-label / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[1].children[0].children[3].children[1] (component: alert-dialog-trigger)
  'alert-dialog-trigger' contains no 'button' to carry the required content.
  Why: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.
Violation 8: [rule.trigger-carries-label / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[2].children[3].children[1] (component: alert-dialog-trigger)
  'alert-dialog-trigger' contains no 'button' to carry the required content.
  Why: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.
Violation 9: [rule.trigger-carries-label / must]
  At: $.root.children[1].children[3].children[1] (component: alert-dialog-trigger)
  'alert-dialog-trigger' contains no 'button' to carry the required content.
  Why: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.

A correct example (ex.delete-account-confirmation): {"dspackSurface":"0.1","system":"shadcn/ui","intent":"destructive-action","root":{"component":"card","children":[{"component":"alert-dialog","children":[{"component":"alert-dialog-trigger","children":[{"component":"button","props":{"variant":"destructive"},"text":"Delete account"}]},{"component":"alert-dialog-content","children":[{"component":"alert-dialog-title","text":"Delete your account?"},{"component":"alert-dialog-description","text":"This will permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone."},{"component":"alert-dialog-footer","children":[{"component":"alert-dialog-cancel","text":"Cancel"},{"component":"alert-dialog-action","text":"Delete account"}]}]}]}]}}

Remove or restructure whatever is necessary to fix the violations, including deleting the offending elements; preserve unaffected content where possible.
Respond with a single JSON object conforming to the provided schema. No commentary.
